prompt: A MedVoyage Provider & Trip Enrollment system with main features including offline clinician credentialing, group medical trip signup with seat locking, membership purchasing and billing reconciliation, internal payment and settlement handling, advanced search and recommendations, and end-to-end auditability. The interface is built with Laravel + Livewire so staff can complete the full workflow in a responsive, form-driven web UI without any internet connection: Patients and members sign in with a locally verified username and password, browse offerings using keyword search plus filters such as specialty, trip date, difficulty level, estimated duration, and prerequisites, then sort by most booked, newest, or highest rated. The UI provides type-ahead suggestions from locally stored terms and a personal search history panel that can be cleared at any time. For group trips, users complete a guided signup form, see real-time seat availability, and receive immediate feedback when a seat is temporarily held; holds expire after 10 minutes of inactivity and automatically release back to inventory, while a waitlist queue is shown when capacity is reached. Members can purchase or renew plans, apply price-difference top-ups within 30 days of purchase, manage invoicing details for statements, and request full or partial refunds; finance staff can reconcile invoices and variances, void incorrect charges, and export statements to a local file for bookkeeping. Credentialing staff upload doctor documents (license, board certification, CV) and move cases through an initial review and re-review process, with clear “request more materials” and rejection paths before activation. On the system side, the backend uses Laravel to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by Livewire components, with PostgreSQL for durable, local persistence and transactional integrity. Key tables (trip signups, membership orders, payments, settlements, reviews) enforce optimistic locking using row versioning; every write operation accepts an idempotency key so duplicate submissions (double-clicks, retries) do not create duplicate orders, refunds, or approvals. A tamper-evident audit log is recorded for create/update/approve/export actions, capturing operator identity, timestamp, workstation IP, and before/after change details, with searchable traceability by user, date range, record type, and correlation ID. “Payments” are handled as offline tender recording rather than external gateways: cash/check/card-on-file entries are captured, callbacks are not network-based but modeled as internal confirmation events that still must pass idempotency validation; settlements batch close daily at 11:59 PM local time and generate a locally stored statement, with reconciliation flows flagging exceptions when totals diverge beyond $0.01. Sensitive fields (SSN fragments if present, license numbers, addresses) are encrypted at rest using application-managed keys and masked on screen by role, while uploaded files are stored locally with size limits (max 10 MB), PDF/JPEG/PNG validation, and checksum fingerprinting to detect duplicates; access is controlled by role-based permissions for Member, Doctor, Credentialing Reviewer, Finance Specialist, and System Administrator. Recommendation logic is implemented as pluggable, local strategy classes (e.g., “most booked in last 90 days,” “similar specialty”) so the organization can swap ranking rules without any external services.
